zh.Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭倫敦可拉肯維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Quincy, the Younger於大約1180年出生在英國哈普爾維古姆時，他的父親Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester21歲，母親Margaret de Beaumont27歲。他和Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln至少有1個女兒。他去世於1217年在英國倫敦享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦可拉肯維爾。

zh-hans.Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kin: 英国英格兰倫敦可拉肯維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Quincy, the Younger于大约1180年出生在英国哈普爾維古姆时，他的父亲Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester21岁，母亲Margaret de Beaumont27岁。他和Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln至少有1个女儿。他去世于1217年在英国倫敦享年38岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦可拉肯維爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 鄭, possibly based on its Hokkien or Teochew pronunciation, see   Zheng   1.

variants.name: Zheng
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jian, Yi, Bin, Hong, Hua, Li, Wei, Yong, Ting, Xin, Yan, Hui, Min, Hao, Shen, Da, Quan, Tian, Chong.

Mandarin form of the surname 鄭: from Zheng (鄭), the name of a state (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) granted to You, the younger brother of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). The state of Zheng was annexed by the state of Han (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Younger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Northumberland and eastern Scotland): from Middle English yungre ‘younger’, comparative of Young , used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In one case, at least, however, the name is known to have been borne by an immigrant Fleming, and was probably an Americanized form of Middle Dutch jongheer ‘young nobleman’ (see Jonker ).
Americanized form of various cognate or like-sounding names in other languages, notably German   Junger   and   Junker  , or Dutch   Jonker  .

variants.name: Young
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: nickname from Middle English yong ‘young’ (Old English geong), used to distinguish a younger man from an older man bearing the same personal name (typically, father and son). In Middle English this name is often found with the Anglo-Norman French definite article, for example Robert le Yunge. In Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland this was widely used as an English equivalent of the Gaelic nickname Og ‘young’; see Ogg . This surname is also very common among African Americans.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘young’ or similar, notably German   Jung  , Dutch   Jong   and   De Jong  , and French   Lejeune   and   Lajeunesse  .
Americanized form of Swedish Ljung: topographic or an ornamental name from ljung ‘(field of) heather’, or a habitational name from a placename containing this word, e.g. Ljungby.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Quincy, the Younger was born about 1180, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, his father, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, was 21 and his mother, Margaret de Beaumont, was 27. He had at least 1 daughter with Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. He died in 1217, in London, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Quincy, the Younger est né environ 1180, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Beaumont, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. Il est décédé en 1217, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Clerkenwell, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Quincy, the Younger um 1180 in Winchester, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester 21 und seine Mutter Margaret de Beaumont 27. Er hatte mit Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1217 im Alter von 38 Jahren in London, England und wurde in Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Quincy, the Younger è nato il circa 1180 nel Winchester, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Beaumont, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. Morì nel 1217, nel London, England, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolto a Clerkenwell, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Quincy, the Youngerが約1180年にイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターで生まれた時，父Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchesterは21歳，母Margaret de Beaumontは27歳であった。彼はHawise de Chester Countess of Lincolnとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1217年にイギリスロンドンで38歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンクラーケンウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Quincy, the Younger(이)가 대략 1180년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Beaumont(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1217년 영국 런던에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 클레르켄웰에 묻혔다.

mn.Clerkenwell, London, England, United Kin: Clerkenwell, London, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Quincy, the Younger nasceu aproximadamente 1180, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Beaumont, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. Ele faleceu em 1217, em London, Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Clerkenwell, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Quincy, the Younger родился около 1180 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Beaumont, 27. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. Он умер в 1217 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности клиркенвелл, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Quincy, the Younger nació aproximadamente 1180, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margaret de Beaumont, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Hawise de Chester Countess of Lincoln. Murió en 1217, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Clerkenwell, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
